summary: Tight show highlighted by a massive He’s Gone jam and soaring Truckin’
blurb: The second set flows seamlessly from Shakedown Street into Greatest Story Ever Told, launches into an expansive He’s Gone jam, and builds to a high‑energy Truckin’ that weaves back into Space. Jerry’s guitar is loud and crystal‑clear, Brent’s keyboards dominate the mix, and the rhythm section drives the improvisations. The performance ranks among the stronger 1981 outings.
review: On November 29, 1981 at Pittsburgh’s Civic Arena the Dead delivered a tight, high‑octane show that still feels fresh. Set two is the highlight: a flawless segue from Shakedown Street into Greatest Story Ever Told opens a monster He’s Gone jam, which blossoms through an inspired Drums‑Space excursion before erupting into a soaring Truckin’ that pulls the crowd right back into the groove. Jerry Garcia’s guitar cuts through with both power and nuance—his tone is described as “loud, clear” and “crystal‑clear” on the best matrix capture—while Brent Mydland’s keyboards are front‑and‑center, adding depth and realism to every song. Bob Weir’s vocal contributions, including a memorable whistle moment, add extra color, and the twin drummers (Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann) provide that classic improvisational space that makes the second‑set flow feel seamless. Standout moments also include a punchy Satisfaction, a soulful Candyman, and a heartfelt Althea, all wrapped up with a warm encore of U.S. Blues.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All four recordings are marked positive; the soundboard review praises multiple standout songs and a tight set flow, while audience and matrix tapes echo the show’s energy and clarity. Consistent language such as “excellent,” “tight,” “massive He’s Gone jam,” and “crystal‑clear” across sources signals strong positive sentiment, justifying a moderate‑high adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard source with crisp clarity, prominent Brent keyboards and loud Jerry guitar; captures the massive He’s Gone jam and high‑energy Truckin’ without dropouts.

key_highlights: Massive He's Gone jam leading into Drums and Space
Seamless Shakedown Street > Greatest Story Ever Told transition
Standout performances of Satisfaction, Truckin' and Candyman
Brent Mydland’s keyboard textures are prominent and richly detailed

band_performance.Jerry: Loud, clear and nailing his parts; guitar tone praised as crystal‑clear and powerful throughout the set.
band_performance.Bob: Vocal contributions stand out, adding flavor (including a noted whistle on a song).
band_performance.Brent: Keyboards are prominent, deep and realistic; several reviewers call out his presence as a highlight of the night.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums segment showcases classic improvisational space, contributing to the expansive He’s Gone/Space flow.
band_performance.Billy: Together with Mickey drives the rhythmic foundation; the Drums‑Space section is praised for its energy and cohesion.
